characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: With sweet and shy Cleo as Beauty, smart and responsible Emma as Brains, and tomboyish Rikki as Brawn. When Emma leaves in season 3, Cleo eventually went into Brains territory whileDude MagnetBella took over the Beauty spot. Blessed with Suck: For our heroines, contact with water is not always a pleasant thing. Given how much water exists in day-to-day life, the girls are always a hair's breadth from being discovered as mermaids. Childhood Friends: Emma and Cleo are childhood friends, and both meet new girl Rikki in the first episode. Bella is the new friend when Emma leaves in season 3. Fanservice Pack: Season 2 has the girls wearing a lot more figure flattering clothing and sexier outfits, especially for Cleo. Making a Splash: All of them have varying powers and abilities over water.

characters.name: Cleo Sertori
characters.description: Ambiguously Brown: Has a bit of a deeper complexion that none of the rest of her family have. Animal Lover: While she can be shy and awkward around people, Cleo has a deep love for animals, shown in how lovingly she takes care of her fish tank and of the dolphins at her theme park job, as well as her rescue of a pelican and several turtles. Betty and Veronica: The Betty to Charlotte's Veronica and Lewis' Archie, having known Lewisfor nearly all their lives, being shy and usually sweet and not very forward about her feelings. Blow You Away: Season 2 has her powers increase, giving her the ability to manipulate the wind. Can't Spit It Out: Her feelings for Lewis are plain on sight for everyone throughout Season 1, and after they've broken up in Season 2, it'sagainobvious that she isn't over him, but she can't get herself to just tell him that for the longest time.

characters.name: Emma Gilbert
characters.description: An Ice Person: Her water-powers involve frozen water particularly. Blue Blood: Downplayed; she comes from a family of white collar workers. Control Freak: A pretty good example of this is when Juicenet got a new manager who started making changes around the cafe, some of which were pretty sensible (e.g., removing a product from the menu that wasn't selling, and rearranging the cold room so that the fresh fruit wouldn't freeze and thus lose flavor). Rather than try to open a dialogue about it and try to negotiate and compromise, Emma just tried to sabotage him to get him fired. Cool Big Sis: Her little brother Elliot has absolute trust in her and looks up to her. Defrosting Ice Queen: She's incredibly uptight early on in the series, but does mellow out.

characters.name: Rikki Chadwick
characters.description: Back for the Finale: An adult Rikki provides the onlyRequired Spinoff Crossoverin the two-part finale of daughter showMako Mermaids: An H₂O Adventure, where she's become a renowned maritime salvager by secretly using her mermaid powers. She's surprised to discover theMako Mermaidsprotagonists are real mermaids, as that's very much outside context for her home series, and resolves to try to get back in touch with her friends. Boring, but Practical: One of the most frequent uses of her heat based powers is to evaporate any water touching her or her friends to quickly undo the mermaid transformation or to stop it from happening in the first place. Burning with Anger: She has anger issues and fire powers. Especially highlighted in her full moon episode, when her inability to control her powers is a metaphor for her inability to control her emotions. Deadpan Snarker: In one interview Cariba Heine said that Rikki's dry one liners was the thing she liked the most about her character. Establishing Character Moment: Her first scene in the first episode has her jump into a boat from the dock to help Cleo after Cleo gets set adrift by Zane. She then reveals that she stole the sparkplug from Zane's because she doesn't like him. This establishes her as a fearless rebel with a dislike ofJerk Jocktypes and little regard for personal property, but still a basically decent person at heart.

characters.name: Bella Hartley
characters.description: Dude Magnet: Lampshaded when Rikki tells Bella that she only likes Will because "he's the only guy in a five-mile radius not to swoon over [her]". Everyone Loves Blondes: As mentioned inDude Magnetabove, she attracts a lot of attention from guys. On her first appearance on the show, she immediately caught the attention of a group of dudes at the beach. Unfortunately these guys proceed to start catcalling her, but Cleo and Rikki are quick to come to her rescue. Nate is also stunned by Bella as soon as he lays eyes on her. Genki Girl: Bella is described as being a bubbly ball of energy who's easy to talk to. Idol Singer: She is a singer and performs at Rikki's cafe. Sixth Ranger: Joins Cleo and Rikki after Emma leaves.

characters.name: LewisMcCartney
characters.description: Absent-Minded Professor: He can get a bit wrapped up in his experiments and studies. It cost him the chance to ask Cleo to the school dance, and also plays a part in their relationship going awry as Lewis fixates on trying to figure out the girls' magical powers instead of giving Cleo the sort of emotional support she needs. Back for the Finale: Written out halfway through Season 3, he manages to come visit Cleo in the final moments of the series finale. Betty and Veronica: The Archie to Cleo's Betty and Charlotte's Veronica. Childhood Friend Romance: He and Cleo have known each other since their ages were in the single digits, and they're clearly in love with each other in the present day. Deadpan Snarker: He gives as many good zingers as Rikki does, to the point where the two of them can engage inPassive-Aggressive Kombatwhen they interact.

characters.name: Will Benjamin
characters.description: Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: He’s blonde haired boy, but he is genuinely good-hearted and friendly. I Just Want to Be Normal: Shades of this, as we find out that he isn't so interested in being a world-class competition diver. Loving a Shadow: He admits that his attraction to Bella is because of what she is (a mermaid), not who she is. Mistaken for Cheating: In Series 3. Will unintentionally stood up on Bella’s date with him because something came up until Bella suspects Will with another girl (who is a red-head woman). But Bella find out that red-haired woman is just his sister, Sophie. Secret Chaser: Encountering the water tentacle in the Moon Pool causes him to become curious about the pool, and the girls who came to his rescue. He eventually notices that Bella flees from water, and manages to force her secret out of her by splashing her when she can't easily run away from him.

characters.name: Miriam Kent
characters.description: Alpha Bitch: Miriam is the school's rich, popular girl, andnota nice person. Lampshaded by Kim in "Something's Fishy"."Miriam'salwaysthe leader." Arch-Enemy: While she bullies all three of the girls, she tends to antagonize Cleo the most as herShrinking Violetnature makes her an easy target. The Bully: Miriam is the meanest teenager in season 1. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Also disappeared after season 1 with no explanation.

characters.name: Dr. Denman
characters.description: Beauty Is Bad: A strikingly beautiful woman, and she kidnaps four teenagers and threatens them with bodily harm for her own profit. Create Your Own Villain: Denman was just a regular old world famous marine biologist minding her own business until Lewis tried to use her resources for his mermaid research. Head-Turning Beauty: Lewis lampshades that she looks more like a model than a scientist. Karma Houdini: Let's recount Dr. Denman's crimes: Four counts of kidnapping, with the intent of forcibly experimenting on three people, and threatening Lewis' life to keep the girls in line. No legal trouble, no harm done to her career, nothing. No one other than a few people ever find out, so not even her reputation suffers. She just...doesn't get to do the experiments she wanted.To be fair, they couldn't exactly have her arrested without explaining why she would kidnap them. To be fair, they couldn't exactly have her arrested without explaining why she would kidnap them.

characters.name: Charlotte Watsford
characters.description: All Your Powers Combined: Has the powers of Emma, Cleo and Rikki all in one, as she went into the pool on her own at the full moon where the three girls were in the pool together. Betty and Veronica: The Veronica to Cleo's Betty, the confident, artistically interested new girl who openly flirts with Lewis and hasa bit of an edge to her attitude. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:Subtle for the most part of the season, but she is spiteful to Cleo as soon as their rivalry over Lewis becomes clear; this includes intentionally making Cleo look bad in front of her family or Lewis, rummaging through her things without permissionnoteand making a move like she wants to steal Cleo's necklace, and ever again intentionally rubbing in that she's spending time with Lewis — all with a sugary sweet smile on her face.Less subtly, her relationship with Lewis has abusive traits, such as her following him everywhere (including when he's at the house of one of the girls), declining phone calls for him without his knowledge, bossing him around and guilt-tripping him when he wants to spend time with anyone but her. Subtle for the most part of the season, but she is spiteful to Cleo as soon as their rivalry over Lewis becomes clear; this includes intentionally making Cleo look bad in front of her family or Lewis, rummaging through her things without permissionnoteand making a move like she wants to steal Cleo's necklace, and ever again intentionally rubbing in that she's spending time with Lewis — all with a sugary sweet smile on her face. Less subtly, her relationship with Lewis has abusive traits, such as her following him everywhere (including when he's at the house of one of the girls), declining phone calls for him without his knowledge, bossing him around and guilt-tripping him when he wants to spend time with anyone but her.

characters.name: Sophie Benjamin
characters.description: Big Sister Instinct: She pushes her brother Will to excel in diving, believing she has his bests interests at heart. Evil Redhead: She wants to support herself and her brother, but her greed becomes her downfall. Manipulative Bitch: She uses Zane and Rikki to get the manager position, and tries to control her brother's relationship with Bella. Relationship Sabotage: Her flirting with Zane eventually destroys Zane and Rikki's relationship. Took a Level in Kindness: When Mako Island undergoes an avalanche, her brother Will asks her which means more to her - either him or the money. She then takes Will's side and escapes. At the school graduation, she presumably apologizes to the girls and becomes on better terms with him and her brother.

characters.name: Zane Bennett
characters.description: Heel Realization: When Dr. Denman has caught the mermaids with Zane's and Mr. Bennett's help, Zane's triumph over having his mermaid theory proven only lasts moments as he finds his girlfriend and her friends imprisoned, and quickly turns against the ruthless scientists. Hidden Depths: Season 2 reveals that he takes an interest in motorcycles, both for sports and the mechanical aspects. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Starts out as an arrogant and bullying bad boy, but he really falls in love with Rikki and eventually shows his nice and caring side. Lonely Rich Kid: He's very rich and has a complicated relationship with his father. Secret Chaser: He glimpses Emma's mermaid form in "Shipwrecked" and spends a good deal of time afterwards trying to find the supposed sea monster.

characters.name: Byron
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappeared after Season 1 with no explanation.

characters.name: Ash
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappears after season 2, most likely because of Emma beingPut on a Bus. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: To Byron as Emma'sLove Interest.

characters.name: Tiffany
characters.description: Beta Bitch: She's almost as mean as Miriam. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappears after season 1. Hopeless Suitor: She has a crush on Lewis, who is blatantly in love with Cleo. Throw the Dog a Bone: After spending the entirety of "Something Fishy" taking Miriam's abuse, she gets crowned the winner of the Miss Sea Queen pageant after Miriam gets disqualified. Token Minority: Is one of the few minorities we see during the show.

characters.name: Louise Chatham
characters.description: Ambiguous Situation: She claims to have given up being a mermaid, though subsequent material would later show that it's actually extremely difficult to break the enchantment, and only Gracie was confirmed to have used the fifty-year full moon on herself. On top of that, she still seems to have supernatural abilities, yet she's never seen getting wet in the present day, leaving it unknown if she still has her powers or not. Cloudcuckoolander: She appears to be one of these. It could possibly be an early stage version of dementia. Eccentric Mentor: She's trying to mentor the girls the way she and her friends weren't. Her habit of being cryptic and disappearing when no one is looking at her tend to frustrate them instead, especially Rikki. Eventually deconstructed as her eccentricity gets her in legal trouble with Zane.

characters.name: Gracie
characters.description: I Just Want to Be Normal: Gave up her powers as she missed being an ordinary girl with no major secrets to keep.

characters.name: Julia
characters.description: Out of Focus: Out of the three original mermaids no much is known about her later life seeing as she died offscreen in Season 1 which lead to Rikki inheriting her locket.

characters.name: Max Hamilton
characters.description: Did Not Get the Girl: Gracie was convinced that he loved the mermaid half of her more, and broke up with him while giving up her powers. The Smart Guy: He was the Lewis analogue for the previous generation, and has finding about Mako Island that he refuses to share with the world.
